title: Catarata Río Fortuna
text: Beautiful waterfall in the middle of the rainforest. A must visit. The long hike was worth the refreshment offered relaxing in the pools below enjoying the natural beauty.

title: Catarata Río Fortuna
text: There were lots of stairs so it was a great way to get in an excellent workout. Thankfully it’s built nicely with seating areas to take a rest on the way up or down. It cost $20 for each tourist to enter which did feel steep, but glad we got to see it.

title: Catarata Río Fortuna
text: Good, slow going on the climb back up. We are in our 70’s. Took us about 40 minutes to get back to where we started.

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text: It was okay. The menu is too big - Americans, think Cheesecake Factory - and it took a lot longer than we expected considering only one hot dish was ordered. The decor is nice and the staff are trying hard.

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text: Excelente comida y buena atención.

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text: La comida fue fresca y muy deliciosa. La atención fue excelente, siempre amable y oportuna. Sin duda, una experiencia muy agradable que recomendaría.

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text: Food was pretty good but portions were small. Service was lackluster after we didn't order the big spendy steak dishes. Overall I'd say it's over priced for what it is and you can do better elsewhere.

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text: Excelente servicio y calidad.

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text: Buen precio y buena cantidad de comida

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text: Delicious food, excellent service and wonderful experience all in all. We had Bayardo as our server and he was phenomenal and truly made our experience even better!

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text:
Celebrated my birthday here with my wife and it was a wonderful experience. A food menu to suit all tastes. We started with ceviche which was delicious. For the main, I had the churrasco and my wife had the entire snapper. Both portions were huge and very tasty.

To top it all off the wait staff (who were exceptional) surprised me with a birthday song and celebration which was highly memorable.

10/10 experience.

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text: Took a bit longer than anticipated, but still highly recommend. Ceviche was delicious.

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text:
maybe the best decision to eat in La Fortuna.
Food was great, location is amazing and the service was awesome. Thank you Jimmy for the great evening!

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text: Comida riquísima, servicio rápido, personal amable y una área para los niños muy bonita!

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text:
Si buscas un lugar donde comer delicioso en La Fortuna, ¡tienes que venir a Restaurante Fortuneño! Fuimos en familia y quedamos encantados con cada plato que probamos.
Empezamos con el guacamole (fresquísimo) y el ceviche clásico, que tenía el punto de acidez perfecto. De platos fuertes, el churrasco y el lomo estaban en su punto, súper tiernos y con mucho sabor. También probamos los tacos de carnitas y fueron una grata sorpresa, ¡riquísimos!
El ambiente es muy acogedor y familiar, y el servicio fue excelente de principio a fin. Sin duda, es un lugar al que volveremos en nuestra próxima visita. ¡Totalmente recomendado!

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text: Totally blown away by how amazing this place is! We got the mango ceviche and the birria tacos. Also got the fresh watermelon juice and the passion fruit juice with a couple vodka shots in them. 10/10 seriously I’ll be back!!

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text: ¡Comida riquísima! Recomiendo las carnes

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text:
The best fish burger I’ve ever had! If you’re pescatarian, definitely go for it 😋 All their other stuff looked great too! And decor was a wonderful bonus - no wonder they’re always packed 👌🏽
Edit: Went back for the same burger last night, but this time, there was very little cheese, and they completely forgot the guacamole, which is clearly mentioned on the menu - this really complemented the crunchy
fish patty last time. Also, we were craving the fries as last time was so good (I think they were double fried)! Unfortunately, this time, they felt a little undercooked. We thought maybe the last time was a reheating situation, which actually made delicious fries, but then we saw a kid’s burger at the next table with the delicious double fried fries… No idea what happened with our order yesterday, but only taking away 1 star ‘cause if I hadn’t had the first burger, and only had yesterday’s, I’d say it was good. But the last one was WOW! We weren’t even hungry last night, and decided to just share the burger as a little snack because I couldn’t stop thinking about it, but the waiter who took our order seemed to frown when that’s the only food we ordered. Dude, it’s a compliment that we came to a restaurant even when we weren’t hungry 🙄
Bottom line - first time was wow. Last night was good enough, but doesn’t make me wanna go back.

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text: Don’t waste your time. The worst steak I’ve had. Ordered it medium came out well done. The food is mediocre. Personally the regular soda restaurants are 10000x better than this place. Everything was dry. Even drinks were just okay. They also tried charging me 82,000 dollars rather than colones three times before realizing.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: Super schönes Hotel! Die Lage mit Blick auf El Arenal ist traumhaft. Die Pools mit dem heissen Wasser sind eher klein, dafür hat es mehrere. Davon einige mit Sprudelliegen. Das Housekeeping war supernett, Wir hatten tolle Handtuchtiere auf dem Bett und vor der Abreise hat sie uns noch das Auto etwas abgespült, das voller Vogelkot war. Im Resort selber, dass super weitläufig ist, fährt ein Shuttelbus, was mega praktisch ist. Das Restaurant am Abend war lecker und das Frühstücksbuffet genial! Die Poolbar hat sehr feine Pizzas und tolle Burger. Da würd ich wieder hin!

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: We spent an afternoon here with a day pass and we had a great time! It does get a bit packed towards late afternoon especially in the warm pool areas. The onsite pool restaurant is actually excellent with freshly made pizza and flatbread for a very reasonable a la carte price. It was much better and much cheaper than the buffet package.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: Our only regret is not staying longer! The resort is fantastic with beautiful views of the volcano and the valley around the volcano. The pool, slides, and hot springs were clean and we'll maintained. The kids had a blast on the slides and the adults enjoyed the cocktails and swim up bar. There are small cold/hot springs pools scattered around the pool area of the resort, so there is a place for everyone. The room was HUGE, modern and clean with great virws of the area.. The breakfast buffet was fantastic- lots of options for every type of eater.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: Super bien, todo esta super bonito y bien cuidado, increíble experiencia!

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: Surrounded by lush gardens with hot spring, restaurants, bar, and many other amenities right on site. What more could you ask for!

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: Fantastic stay ||We were allocated a room close to reception which worked well for us. The room was spacious and clean. ||The bathroom was large and also clean. ||Room service was fantastic.  ||Breakfast was included and was fantastic. Wife loved the hash browns!!! One one of the mornings we had to leave early for a tour and they provided a breakfast box for us all. ||Kids enjoyed the pools (especially the slides) and the staff (and food) at the pool bar were great!||Would recommend.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text:
Estuve de visita, por la hermosa Costa Rica!
Y a pesar de que estaba lloviendo, se disfrutó mucho el área de las piscinas de aguas termales.
Cenamos allí, y estaba muy buena.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: The resort was large and well-maintained. We stayed in one of the buildings far from the amenities, so you had to either walk a long way, drive, or take a shuttle. Although we originally didn't like being that far, we grew to like it because it was quiet and peaceful. |We had a nice terrace from which we could probably see the volcano if it was not clouded over the entire time. But we also saw lots of birds, wild pigs, and a large band of koatis that stayed around all the time. |Breakfast buffett was ample. We also ate lunch and dinner at the restaurant, and were pleased with the food quality. |The pools were very nice but crowded, though, as they sell day passes to outside guests. The pool with a slide was not heated, so our kids were disappointed and chose not to get into the cold water. |It rained a lot, so things don't dry on the terrace. We used a clothesline strung in the bathroom to dry our clothes. |This resort is far from town, so you definitely need a car to go anywhere. But they had zip-lining and horse riding on the premises.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: Die Bewertung betrifft den Day Pass: exzellentes Preis-Leistungsverhältnis, kleinere Kinder sind kostenlos. Selbst an Ostern war es nicht überfüllt. Sehr schöne Anlage!

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text:
-The resort itself and it's ponds+ gardening are A++ level. Really amazing with a lot of attention to details.
- Location and views are amazing.
- the rooms are mid for Western standards but big and functional.
- the food is varied but mid, expensive with ok service.

My family of 4 enjoyed this resort

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: Dieses ist vergleichbar mit einem  Clubhotel in Europa. Für Familien perfekt. Man kann den ganzen Tag baden und die Kids fühlen sich wohl. ||Bei der Anreise war La Fortuna atemberaubend! Später war er in Nebel gehüllt. ||Wir hatten ein Reihenendhaus am Berg. Das Zimmer war sehr groß, gefiel uns aber nicht so gut: Die Vordächer waren sehr breit und durch das einzige Fenster im Wohnraum kam kaum Licht hinein. Das WC verfügte über das zweite Fenster, was aber sehr klein war. Im Bad fehlte eine Entlüftung, daher war die Raumluft sehr feucht und unangenehm. Es gab eine Terrassentür (ohne Fenster) sodass man querlüften konnte.||Insgesamt hätten in dem Zimmer wohl acht Personen schlafen können, was wohl auch einige gemacht haben. ||Das Frühstück war ok, beim Abendessen hatte ich mir den Magen verdorben. Das war nicht so schön und brachte zwei Tage Bauchschmerzen mit sich. ||Die Pools waren durch die warmen Quellen sehr angenehm und insgesamt schön gestaltet.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: The hot springs were amazing, I liked the variety of different pools.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text:
I booked this place because of all the strong reviews, but our experience was specific to one of the villas, which runs about $1,000 USD a night.

The main reason we chose the villa was the “fully equipped kitchen,” especially since breakfast isn’t included. In reality, it’s pretty basic. You get pots and pans, but no dish towels, no colander, no cheese grater, just little things you’d expect at this price point that weren’t there. It made cooking more frustrating than it needed to be.

The villa itself is big and clean. You’ll get the odd bug, but that’s expected in the jungle and wasn’t a big deal. The pool is more like a hot tub in size and temperature, but it does have a really nice view of the volcano.

One thing that stood out in a bad way, my daughter put on goggles in the pool and came up with a few toenails in the water. Not exactly something you want to see.

The bigger issue for us was trust. We ended up leaving a day early after realizing a staff member had taken a flashlight from our room and claimed it as her own, even though our last name was engraved on it. When we brought it up, she gave it back right away, but it didn’t sit well.

To be fair, the manager was easy to deal with and seemed to understand our concerns, which we appreciated.

Overall, the views are great and the place has potential, but for what you’re paying, it didn’t really meet expectations for us.

title: Catarata Río Fortuna

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text:
I came here for dinner with a fellow solo traveller and had a really great experience.

I ordered the watermelon and feta salad followed by the chicken cordon bleu with mashed potatoes and vegetables, and everything was absolutely delicious. The flavours were well balanced and the food felt fresh and well prepared.

The atmosphere was relaxed, making it a nice place to enjoy a meal in the evening.

Overall, a really enjoyable dinner and I would definitely recommend this restaurant.

Get there early for dinner as queues start to form!

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text:
Restaurante Fortuneno is a solid spot if you’re looking for a casual meal with a mix of lighter bites and classic dishes.

The pineapple juice was one of the highlights—fresh, sweet, and really refreshing. It had that natural tropical flavor that pairs perfectly with the warm weather in La Fortuna.

For the food, the appetizer quesadillas were a good choice for something light. They were simple but satisfying, with a nice balance of melted cheese and a lightly crisped tortilla. The shrimp tempura (listed under a different name on the menu) was crispy on the outside and tender inside, making it a great shareable dish.

The fries were well-cooked and complemented the meal nicely. The steak, while a more straightforward dish, was filling and flavorful enough to round everything out.

Overall, Restaurante Fortuneno offers a relaxed dining experience with refreshing drinks and a variety of easy, enjoyable options

title: Restaurante Fortuneño

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text:
Such an amazing hotel. The staff is super nice the rooms very clean and spacious.

10/10 would definitely recommend!
Just note that the water slide is very very fast and a little dangerous for adults.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: We rented the Villa March 22 & 23, 2026. This place is amazing and much nicer than advertised. There are 3 bedrooms 4 beds that all have trundles and 2 full bathrooms. The private plunge pool is the best. This is really a giant hot tub heated with jets that overlooks the Arenal volcano, the views are unbelievable. The warm water is just what is needed in the cool air and so relaxing after an all day hike. There were a pair of Toucans living in the trees just above the pool, it was so cool to watch them. The restaurant has pretty good food, snacks and bar type food is available at the pool which was good as well. The drinks are good with very low prices. The small hot spring fed pools are awesome and include a swim up bar! Here is the most amazing thing... we left an ipad out on the porch and had traveled to Tamarindo for a few days. After contacting them they sent this to us, same day, personally delivered 4 hours away in Tamarindo. Of course we had to pay 70$ but totally worth it and we were so grateful that this was an option. Would definitely stay here again!

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: We stayed here during spring break trip with our older children. We stayed two nights in rooms on the road above the pools. The rooms were nice and had outdoor area but no view. The pools are the reason I wanted to write a review: they were amazing! And better maintained than any pools I've seen elsewhere: fully drained and thoroughly cleaned every night. Of course that means when they close at 10pm they are really closed. They open again at 10am, but since some are filled earlier, you can go in once they're available.||One downside as a resort guest is that the pools are available for use by day trippers. So they get busier as those people arrive.||And the pools are all ages, so be prepared for little ones wherever they pop up. There are pools for the little tikes, but they aren't limited to those pools.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text:
The resort was advertised as a luxury resort, but my experience did not match that expectation. The pools were poorly maintained, which was disappointing considering the price point and the image they promote. The wet bar area was also full of children, which did not seem appropriate since this is typically considered an adults-oriented space.
Another issue was the towel policy. Initially, we were told the cost would be 24 €, which seemed excessive and unpleasant, but later we found out it was actually 4 €. While 4 € is reasonable, the initial misinformation created a negative first impression.
Additionally, the service at the wet bar was not good. Some staff members were rude and appeared more willing to serve guests from the all-inclusive program before those of us with day passes, which made us feel like second-class customers.
Overall, the experience did not reflect what I would expect from a resort marketed as luxury.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: Ready to come back in a heartbeat—we truly loved everything about Hotel Los Lagos. The grounds are beautiful and well cared for, and the hot springs are simply amazing—such a perfect place to relax.|The food at both restaurants was consistently fresh and delicious. We especially enjoyed the Lemnos Wet Bar and found ourselves going back there often. Every employee we encountered was friendly and welcoming, and a special shoutout to Gustavo at the wet bar for going above and beyond.|It’s clear that Hotel Los Lagos has put a lot of thought into creating a great guest experience. We especially appreciated the convenient towel service and the spacious rooms that comfortably sleep six. It all added up to a memorable and easy, enjoyable vacation.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text:
I went to Los Lagos hot springs on the Day Pass. 33$ per person.
The site is amazing! Gorgeous landscaping, stunning view of the volcano and so many little pools to choose from. The thermal pools are all different temperatures and there is signage for each one. So many pools to discover, it’s easy to find a little spot all to yourself. The water-jet massage beds are nice too.
Be sure to take time to explore the property and drive up to the mirador, the view is spectacular!

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text:
Die Anlage ist sehr schön, viele tolle Pflanzen, mit kleinen Wegen angelegt, tropisch. Als wir dort waren, war es angenehm, aber wenn es mal voll wird, dann kann es sehr laut und unpersönlich werden. Der Hauptpool ist mit der Poolbar viel zu laut und viel zu voll, man kann dort nicht entspannen. Schade. Die Temperaturangaben an den Becken sind nicht korrekt. Es gibt einen Handtuchservice (2 Dollar pro Handtuch), und kostenlose Schließfächer. Bei Regen gibt es allerdings nicht viel Überdachung, Handtücher werden nass und es ist sehr beengt in den wenigen überdachten Becken. Es gibt in den Umkleiden keine Föhne! Getränkepreise sind einigermaßen ok, und die Drinks dürfen mit in den Pool genommen werden. Großer Nachteil: es gibt kein kostenloses Wasser (ist in anderen Einrichtungen üblich)! Es gibt in den Becken wenig Sitzmöglichkeiten, daher ist es schwierig mit dem entspannt hinlegen oder hinsetzen, da sollte auf jeden Fall nachgearbeitet werden.
Restaurant: Büffet ist klein, aber ok, es ist aber das Geld nicht wert, was man zusätzlich für Lunch/Dinner zahlt. Nicht gemütlich, eher Kantinenflair. Auf Anfrage gab es ein Glas Wasser, aber niemand hat gefragt, ob wir was trinken möchten. Sehr unmotiviertes und unfreundliches Personal.

title: Catarata Río Fortuna
text:
A wonderful place full of nature and life. You need to go through 585 steps down and up, in the middle of the rain forest, but is worth it, once you get to the waterfall.

If the weather is good enough you will be able to swim in the cold waters of the river if not, you can always take a good picture next to the amazing waterfall. It is not accessible for wheelchair.

100 percent recommended if you want a have an amazing view after an easy hike.

Note: A) Be ready for going up and down the stairs. Its not going to be easy with kids. B) Take water shoes C) Take a phone waterproof cover if you want to take photos while you are within the waterfall D) Change into your towels and Swim wear before you go to the waterfall E) Get change of clothes.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text:
My husband and I did not stay at the hotel but purchased two day passes to visit the hot springs and have dinner. While I’ve personally visited other hot springs in La Fortuna that I found more aesthetically pleasing, I specifically chose this location because of the breathtaking viewpoint I had seen on TikTok.

When we arrived, we purchased two day passes with dinner included for $55 each. We were given a dinner voucher and a map of the resort and told to have a wonderful time. Unfortunately, the map repeatedly led us in the wrong direction when we tried to find the viewpoint. After about 30 minutes of searching, we asked for help and were then told the viewpoint was far up the hill.

We walked for about 45 minutes uphill before a hotel shuttle driver kindly stopped and took us a little further. He didn’t speak much English, so he couldn’t tell us how much farther we had to go. About ten minutes later we encountered a gentleman jogging up the hill who told us the viewpoint was another mile and a half away. At that point we realized that experiencing the viewpoint realistically requires a car. After walking for nearly an hour and a half, we eventually gave up and headed back down. It was extremely disappointing because that viewpoint was the main reason I chose this hot springs.

Once we returned, we went to the restaurant to use our dinner vouchers. When we arrived around 5:30 pm, the hostess told us the buffet ended at 6 pm but that we should wait, which was confusing. I thought maybe there was a language barrier but she spoke english perfectly fine to the other guests. This prompted me to ask again, and  we were finally allowed in and offered seating in a secluded corner. When I asked if we could sit somewhere else, I was told the other tables were reserved for guests who had made reservations and not for those with vouchers. However, during dinner we noticed other guests with vouchers arriving after 6 pm, choosing their seating freely, and still being allowed to eat. It was uncomfortable, and I hate to think we were singled out, especially since we were the only Black guests there.

After dinner, I asked the lobby if we could briefly return the next morning before our flight just to capture the viewpoint photo we had come for, but we were told we would have to purchase another day pass.

Overall, I left feeling pretty disappointed with the experience. Thankfully, my husband encouraged me to make the best of the evening and enjoy the hot springs, which did end up being the highlight of the night. The bartender was incredibly welcoming and kind.

I’ll be returning to La Fortuna in July and hope to finally capture that beautiful viewpoint. I truly hope the experience is different next time.

title: Catarata Río Fortuna
text: We had an incredible experience visiting this place during the low season in April. We drove ourselves and found ample parking spots. We bought our tickets on-site, and there was no wait at all. They provided us with a waiver and explained that there are restrooms, a shop, a restaurant, and an ice cream parlor (with only two flavors) and a bar. There’s a 500-step hike down and up. The water is freezing, but the view is breathtaking. We thoroughly enjoyed our time here. I recommend bringing briny water shoes for the rocks are slippery once you’re in the water. Also, if you sweat easily, bring a small fan.

title: Catarata Río Fortuna
text: Imperdível

title: Catarata Río Fortuna
text: Stunning large waterfall . Walk down 500 steps and a long slow 500 steps back . Well worth the effort . We went on a ‘swimming’ day which was a great experience . Moderately crowded , still plenty of room . Best to bring water shoes .

title: Catarata Río Fortuna
text:
Visitamos a La Fortuna Waterfall e, sem dúvida, foi a melhor experiência da nossa viagem na Costa Rica.

A cachoeira é simplesmente belíssima. A água é incrível, o visual é impressionante e o lugar tem uma energia muito especial. É aquele tipo de passeio que você realmente aproveita com calma, entra na água gelada, vê os peixes, senta nas pedras e curte o momento.

Para chegar até lá, é preciso descer cerca de 500 degraus e depois encarar a subida na volta, mas vale totalmente o esforço. A estrutura do local é boa, com restaurante, banheiros e até algumas lojinhas e opções como sorvetes, tudo pago à parte.

Pagamos cerca de 40 dólares para duas pessoas, o que não é barato, mas dá para entender pelo nível de organização, manutenção das escadas e presença de salva-vidas.

Fomos em um dia de sol lindo, o que deixou tudo ainda mais especial. No geral, foi meu passeio favorito na Costa Rica e eu recomendo muito para quem estiver em La Fortuna.

title: Catarata Río Fortuna
text: It's gorgeous. It rained all day, intermittently, so be prepared for that. Definitely a spot to see.

title: Catarata Río Fortuna
text: This isn't a hike to get away from the crowds.  There is definitely a whole set-up built around this waterfall to accommodate mass people and services.  Nevertheless, it is kind of a must-do hike if visiting La Fortuna (kind of like going to Yellowstone and seeing Old Faithful).  The steps are a plentiful, but easy enough to just take your time coming back up and it was very shaded.  Thought the now PDA sign section was funny.  Naturally, we had to take a selfie of us kissing in front of it before we left.

title: Catarata Río Fortuna
text: Beautiful place to visit but the entrance fee is a little high for what you get, but price point around Costa Rica seems to be typically quite high anyway. Definitely recommend visiting if you’re in the area, but just be wary a lot of the places to visit are pricy and you may have to pick and choose which you feel is most worth visiting

title: Catarata Río Fortuna
text:
Bonita catarata cerca de Arenal y buen tour pero SOBREVALORADO por mucho.
Hay otras cataratas o reservas naturales en el área a las que puedes acceder sin tener que pagar nada e igual de lindas.

title: Catarata Río Fortuna

Es un lugar con buena infraestructura para turistas, pero si buscas una experiencia full naturaleza tipo hike, tal vez no te guste tanto. Tomar en cuenta que son bastantes gradas (no tantas) pero si es una persona con mala condición física, tendrá problemas, pero hay descansaderos

text:

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: Great place to experience the rain forest area in Costa Rica. Be sure to drive up or hike up the mountain from the resort. The view is great. The pools in the resort are great.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: The resort is beautiful with lots of lush greenery, flowers and trees. Each pool or hot springs area is beautiful and close together, yet seperate so you could almost always find a peaceful area to relax. My kids loved the pool and water slides, we all loved the different hot springs and that they were different temperatures. The staff was very helpful any time we needed anything or had questions. The poolside bar and restraunt was a hit with our family. The zipline at the hotel was amazing and we didn't have to leave the hotel to do it. They have a little nature walk by the restraunt, we saw tons of birds, and they have peacocks, turtles, chickens, ducks, fish and beautiful flowers the whole way. The hotel is all on the hillside of the base of the Arenal Volcano area, which gives you beautiful views, but also be prepared for lots of uphill and downhill walking or steps. They do offer the hotel shuttle any time to bring you anywhere on the grounds though if you just call and ask. We saw 2 howler monkeys, a pack of Coati right outside our room, iguanas, and geckos while there too. Any time we wanted to go anywhere that didn't provide transportation we juat messaged the red taxi service and askes for a pickup since we were a group of 6, the Uber's there tend to only take 4 people.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: Property was incredible! Fun for all ages!

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: El lugar es muy agradable y el personal muy amable, puedes hospedarte en el hotel o tener un day pass ($33 sólo el hotel, $57 con alimentos incluidos).

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: Beautiful location and lovely hot springs! The birds and lizards surround you the whole time and the bubble massaging hot spring was the BEST thing I have encountered in my life! The only thing that made this less than a 5-star stay is that our room was WAY atop a steep hill - I am in pretty good shape but I still had sore hammies afterwards!

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: Had a day pass for the hot springs.  This was the best value thing we did the whole trip.  Price was incredible considering what was offered.  Even the food and drink prices were great.  Highly recommend spending the day here.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: Visite las termas como parte de una actividad que contrate. Muy lindo ambiente y decoración , super agradable terminar con un baño relajante en aguas termales y tomar unos drinks en el wet bar. Todo el complejo se ve super chulo.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text:
Magnifique établissement pour passer une journée dans les hot springs, ne pas louper le point de vue sur le volcan au bout de la route, c’est magique
Je déconseille le planteur qui est plutôt une glace à la fraise fondue avec du rhum

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text:

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text:
We had a terrible experience here. We ate dinner one night at their pool bar/ restaurant and got food poisoning. Because of this, we missed our excursion the next day. When we told the front desk about this, they were not at all apologetic. They said that they did “research” and nobody else who ate at the restaurant got sick. They said the best they could offer was 30% off of that meal, which amounted to about 10 USD. After we argued with them, they did end up taking off the entire meal. However, this is the bare minimum they should have done after getting us sick. And the manager should at least apologize. Absolutely horrendous customer service. I would expect much better for the price we paid to stay here. They need to work on their food safety and customer service. After that, maybe they can call this a luxury resort.

We also ate at the other restaurant the night prior and the food was just okay. Would recommend eating at a local place in La Fortuna instead.

The rooms were fine. A bit outdated. No towel hooks, which made it hard to dry stuff. The walls are paper thin, so we could hear everything our neighbours were saying.

The food poisoning experience soured the entire stay. The hot springs were nice, but didn’t make up for it.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: Beautiful resort, friendly staff and great food. Several pools and the resort never felt crowded but BEWARE if your room is in the 200s and therefore higher up on the mountain it is difficult to get around without a car or shuttle service for those traveling with young children or people with reduced mobility! The resort is quite big and has lots of inclines which could make getting around challenging. Nonetheless I would recommend this resort and the volcano views are stunning!

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: Lindo lugar, bonitas y espaciosas habitaciones, 8 piscinas (6 calientes de agua termal y 2 de temperatura ambiente, que era tibias de todas maneras) el lugar es muy grande, tienen buses que te llevan desde la habitación a otras áreas del hotel y viceversa. Las personas muy amables, todo limpio y bonito.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: Did a day pass for the pools. Stayed for about 4/5 hours. Easy to get to. More affordable than other hot springs but you would never know they are any less luxurious. Regular pool and various temperatures of hot pools. Amazing we could go on a Friday night and still have whole pools to ourselves. Wet bar was reasonably priced given its notoriety. You rent the towels with a bigger deposit but thats fine. Lockers are included. Everything felt clean and safe. Bathroom facilities had showers. Beautiful gardens surrounding the pools. Only noteable issue was we, like most people we saw, brought reusable water bottles and there was no where to fill them. Staff suggested using the sink in the bathroom but as a traveler I was a bit weary on that. Probably could just ask the bar.

title: Catarata Río Fortuna
text:
Lo que se dice de los 500 peldaños es cierto, pero totalmente lograble si es que andan apurados para que así puedan ir teniendo leves descansos.
La catarata es hermosa, y lo mejor de todo es que uno se puede bañar ahí. No es para estar un día o mediodia, pero si para disfrutar un momento breve.

title: Catarata Río Fortuna
text: Un bonito lugar para refrescarse de la rutina y compartir con amigos o familia.

title: Catarata Río Fortuna
text:
Fortuna Waterfall is a unique experience. Why do you go to a waterfall? It doesn't seem like it would be a big deal, but it is, or, it can be.

The whole setup is well done. There is a restaurant, bar, ice cream shop, and souvenir shop at the top where you purchase tickets. There is also an observation deck with a lovely view of the valley. My daughter enjoyed a smoothie from the ice cream shop after our hike.

Parking is plentiful, though some of it is up a steep hill. The facility is at the end of a nicely-paved street, a short, easy drive in La Fortuna.

You get down to the waterfall via several sets of stairs, about 530 according to their website. Naturally, that means you ascend the same stairs after visiting the waterfall site. There are plenty of places to catch your breath on the way up. For our part, our son led the charge and we did the stairs in a single effort, something we felt pretty good about.

The waterfall is part of the Fortuna River and its about 70 meters high. As you approach, you notice the thunderous noise. It is loud, but you become used to it and it does not overwhelm.  The area around the falls is rocky and water shoes are a good idea. Navigating over the rocks is tricky, it may go slow.

The overall beauty of the valley is immense. The water is crisp and cool. There is something about waterfall sites. It is a sense of awe, appreciation, and everyone seems to be happy. We experienced this also at La Mina Falls in Puerto Rico.

The La Fortuna Waterfall experience is highly recommended. It is a must if you're in the area. There are many waterfall sites, but you won't find another like this one.

title: Catarata Río Fortuna
text: La cascada es muy bonita pero el precio es excesivo, al menos para los turistas. Son muchas escaleras pero no es complicado.

title: Catarata Río Fortuna

title: Catarata Río Fortuna

No es que esté mal del todo la catarata, pero es más bien una cascada y no tanto una catarata.
A tener en cuenta: son 500 escalones para llegar a verla que luego hay que volver a subir.

title: Catarata Río Fortuna
text:
Una maravilla como otras miles que el turismo y sobre todo el exceso de aforo ha echado a perder.

La cascada sigue siendo espectacular, como cuando estuvimos en 2012. En ese momento estaríamos en toda la cascada unas 15 personas. Probablemente esta vez fueramos 150. Se entiende que el turismo crezca, pero despues de 11 días por Costa Rica es el único sitio en el que hemos sentido que se ha echado a perder.

No hay hueco practicamente para dejar las zapatillas...cuanto menos para acercarte al agua a bañarte. Es tremendo.

es cierto que era festivo y temporada alta, pero....demasiado!!!!

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text: Incredible all around. The tacos al la placha are to DIE for. Also the fish tacos and the chalupitas.

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text: Great food and atmosphere! They have a huge menu with options for anyone. Would recommend if you are in the area.

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text:
Best restaurant and affordable menu we found.
Get there early as it fills up early.

title: Restaurante Fortuneño

text: The server minor took amazing care of us. Me and my friend stayed here for three nights out of the restaurant we tried. This was by far our favorite. It blew every restaurant out of the water.  We are two Canadian girls were sad that we’re gonna go home and not be able to get food like  I recommend getting the combo for two.

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text: Great wait staff.

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text: Atendimento excelente, comida muito saborosa! Carlos foi super atencioso conosco

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text: The most fantastic place in whole Costa Rica! The food was exceptional, staff was very friendly and it had great atmosphere. We ate the best ceviche in our life. Prawn, salmon and tuna were fantastic! We loved it and would definitely recommend this place! While we had our dinner, people were waiting in line, and we would say it is worth waiting. We loved it here!

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text: We saw this spot during the day and thought the place looked amazing with decent prices. We came back and had to wait in line, but the wait was quick and seemed like a fair way to manage demand. When we got to the front we were warned that food may take a long time but we weren’t worried. The food actually took a normal amount of time, but the drinks took longer for some reason… must be how busy it was. We all thought the ambiance and prices met our expectations but the food was okay (not incredible).

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text: Un restaurante con decoración bonita, los platillos tiene un sabor muy rico y es una mezcla de recetas costarricenses con un giro Gourmet. Los precios están un poco elevados, pero vale la pena por el sabor del giro Gourmet que me dan a los platillos; nunca había probado un escabeche de plátano y lo hice ahí y me encantó al igual que el agua de maracuya.

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text: Food was very very delicious. We went with a burrata and and combo that came with ribs,beans, and plantains. Only gripe was service was a little slow. Enough to notice but not enough to ruin the night. We had no wait but after we sat down hella people should up and had to wait in line. We got lucky but you may not so be prepared.

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text: Un lugar muy visitado, la comida es muy buena

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text:
Sobrevalorado.
El tiempo de espera para mesa fue de 10 a 15 minutos a pesar que había mesas.

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text:
Loved this place so much, I went twice during my trip to La Fortuna! Had the tropical ceviche which was so fresh and yummy and a generous helping for one. And then had the ribs combo the second time which were good too (fried plantains were amazing).

There was a long line the first night I was there so be aware there may be a wait!

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text: Service was amazing but the prices were a little steep.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: We only came for the day but the resort is located under the volcano and has a lushious forest surrounding its territory. The entrance fee is fair for the services you get. The resort is clean and has a very friendly staff. I definitely recommend these natural pools for people that want to relax in less crowded but high quality areas🤗

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: You are literally at the base of the volcano.  Wonderful hot pools to lay in and simply relax.  A fabulous  breakfast buffet with everything you could want.  There is a lake just a short drive or walk which is part of the national preserve.   Nice hiking trail there.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: Für diesen Preis unschlagbar. Tolle Touren zum buchen. Viele Hot Tubes zum entspannen und ein tolles Essen. Sehr zum empfehlen.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: Really enjoyed our stay. The hot springs was the highlight of it all! We really enjoyed being able to explore the different pools/springs at all different temperatures. My partner enjoyed the colder temps while I enjoyed the hotter temps, so it was to nice to be able to break off and do our own things at some point. We had a few snacks & drinks at the pool bar and service was fast and friendly. The view of the volcano from the room was breathtaking! The breakfast options had a great variety & changed every single day so you won’t get tired of the same things. We visited in August & it wasn’t packed, never felt like I had to fight for a spot in the springs. The room was clean. Room service was quick and the food was fresh. We had an issue one night with the A/C and the maintenance team came and fixed it immediately. If i were to return to La Fortuna - I would 100% stay here again. The price point is just to good for what you get!

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: It's a huge resort and if there are no clouds location would be perfect.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: The family had an amazing stay at this resort. The property is well kept, the staff are friendly, prices are not exorbitant for a resort, and the hot springs are relaxing and perfect to lounge in, esp on rainy days, as many have coverage. The two pool slides are just pure fun for the kids (and adults). The animals on the ground are fantastic to watch-a guest saw a sloth during breakfast. We initially stayed in a superior room, which was sufficient, then moved to a villa with a private pool, which was amazing. If you are staying for a few days and can splurge on the villa, I highly recommend it. Suggestions for improvement: 1. Another towel hook or rod in the bathrooms; 2. A towel station by the pools with the slides; 3. A toaster and sponge to wash dishes in the villa (breakfast not included). About 8-10min drive to downtown La Fortuna, even closer to Arenal Volcano.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text:
We didn’t stay in the hotel but had a day pass for the hot springs and stayed for dinner in the pool bar.

The hot springs experience was great - good changing rooms and lockers then a range of pools with different water temperatures and sizes. Nice that there’s a swim up bar as well with very reasonably priced drinks. Stayed in for about 2 hrs then changed and sat in the ‘dry’ area of the bar to have a light dinner. Quite a simple menu - we chose to have a plate of nachos each. We were asked if we wanted to have guacamole with it and said yes but later realised this added another 40% to the cost - they should tell you this! Nachos were good and were just the quantity we wanted

Très bel hôtel avec une végétation luxuriante
Personnel aux petits soins

Hot springs pools were marked by temperature and not crowded most times. Safe surroundings.  Family- and couples. The staff took care of us and were great from Reception, service, room and landscaping.

Use a sturdy bathing suit, mine ripped going down.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort

title: Catarata Río Fortuna
text: 1st off......make sure you have some sort of footwear that you what to wear in the water. We were on a tour and we wished that a requirement or suggestion on their website was water shoes. We went across a small river and into the pools closest to the waterfall but not the one the waterfall emptied into because we had barefoot and the rocks were very uncomfortable walking on tours make our way over. Beautiful scenery, cool and refreshing water.

title: Catarata Río Fortuna
text: Absolutely stunning. Visited early at 10am, there was no crowd, we enjoy swimming for a while and got to take some awesome pictures. The restaurant was also delicious

title: Catarata Río Fortuna
text: So awesome, it’s an insane view. Go at opening so you beat the crowds as it can get busy and crowded. We had the place for ourselves for a good bit. We got to see a sloth!!

title: Catarata Río Fortuna
text:
This is the most amazing waterfall we have ever visited. I am sure there are more impressive ones out there but we had never seen one this awesome. Kids loved it. You can go downstream a bit from the main deep pool and there are several child friendly swimming holes.
Make sure you go early. We got there before 730 and had them almost to ourselves.

title: Catarata Río Fortuna
text: 500 steps down weren't bad. It was the 500 back up that were rough. ☺️ Beautiful views. Relaxing.

title: Catarata Río Fortuna
text:
It might look like just a single waterfall. It is a lot more - the water 'explosions' at the bottom is awesome, the vegetation seen close up along  the 500+ steps are interesting, the butterfly garden near the the entrance attracted a couple of humming humming birds.

The waterfall us only 9 mins drive from town. There was no queue when I visited at 3pm.

title: Catarata Río Fortuna
text: Seen many many better waterfalls around the world for free!

title: Catarata Río Fortuna
text: A must stop if you are in Fortuna. It’s an easy walk down to see the waterfall but you might need some breaks in between to get back up. Make sure to bring swimming shorts and water shoes cause it’s worth it… when you’re in the river, be ready to be with fish swimming next to you.

title: Catarata Río Fortuna
text: Amazing waterfalls! Unfortunately, after the heavy rains the water was brown, and the place was extremely crowded - we couldn’t stay more than 10 minutes. Not sure it was worth our time and money.

title: Catarata Río Fortuna
text: Very beautiful experience! Too many steps but well worth it! Water was cold but still able to swim in it! Highly recommended to wear the right shoes if you’re gonna take a dip in the water! $20 per person not too bad!

title: Catarata Río Fortuna
text: Very nice waterfall but a little too expensive, 20$ per person

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: Very difficult to get to the rooms. Not accessible at all.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: Beautiful place. My clients love the viewpoint with volcano views, the beds are amazing, the property feels very exclusive, and the price is very reasonable.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: Un lugar muy bonito para pasar en familia o amigos, el precio es alto, al igual que otros lugares de la zona, pero se disfruta de las instituciones. La comida es muy rica también. Tiene unas piscinas de frías riquísimas y además las muy buscadas aguas termales.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text:
The slide.

The slide is memorable to say the least.

I’m not sure how to give this slide a true honest review. On one hand - you get an unforgettable ride. On the other hand - consider this review your warning.

What looks like an innocent slide quickly changes the minute you start going down. By the first curve, you will have picked up enough speed to start questioning your choice of going down. After the second curve, you get pummeled in the face with water leaving you wondering where you are at and when this will end. Next thing you know, everything goes dark. You are now in a tunnel and have no idea what is happening. One last curve at top speed followed by a 6 ft drop into the water will have you questioning your life choices.

A group of us experienced this slide on our trip and the reviews were mixed (mostly positive). A few people in my group left with chronic back pain and a date with the chiropractor. Others (including me) went down over 100 times in our 2 day stay with limited side effects. 1 person left not being able to hear out of his left ear and I left with a concussion. All of us left with bruises and a memory that will be ingrained in our brain forever.

I would 100% recommend this slide to anyone who enjoys a thrill. There is truly no other slide like it.

For advanced thrill seekers: experience your first ride at night (this is what I did with no warning of what to expect). Woah.

PS: the rest of the resort is awesome too. The rooms are beautiful. Super comfy beds. Great food - the included breakfast is delicious with plenty of options, solid burgers at the bar, calamari was good (but small portion size), the chifrijo was my favorite meal from the restaurant. This place offers amazing hot springs with plenty of different spots to try. If you are lucky, you’ll get a nice view of the volcano too. I’ve tried out quite a few resorts here and this is by far my favorite resort in Costa Rica!

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: Me encantaron las aguas termales

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text:
Did a day pass for the hot springs and honestly it was not great.
Pros: in the evening it seemed there were probably fewer tour buses around although some were still there. The virgin Pina colada I ordered for my kids came in a pineapple which they loved. There were a good number of pools and the architecture allowed you to occasionally have a nook to yourself.
Cons: Kinda dirty. Stuff floating around and some pools just smelled of alcohol. It costs $10 per towel to rent them which you get back but they charge an additional $2 per towel that they keep for a use fee. It seemed pretty steep to me. Lastly, the water slides are downright dangerous. The longer one (ages 10+) is no joke and honestly was to the point it wasn't fun and I was worried I was going to get hurt. The shorter one (ages 6-10) is also dangerous. It's pretty shallow at the bottom so a bigger kid could potentially hit the ground and get hurt as there's a good drop that flings you out. And yes, I did read reviews like this one before going and still thought maybe it wouldn't be that bad and my kids could have fun- but no. This didn't hit the mark for us.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: Muy bonito lugar, ideal para ir en pareja, muy recomendado

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text:
By far, one of our most favorite destinations. Even though it was pretty cloudy we got some glimpses of the volcano that were stunning. Can’t wait to go back when the weather is clearer. There were tons of colorful birds all over the property and the thermal pools were fantastic.

The only thing slightly less than wonderful was the buffet. The buffet was only ok and fine for just a quick breakfast. We ate dinner at The main restaurant once and didn’t care for it so we ended up eating in La Fortuna for the resort of our meals. They did have a great set up for the Super Bowl though right at the swim up bar and the food for that was amazing!

Towel rental was cash only, the ladies working the locker and towel rental were the meanest I’ve encountered on this trip so far.. food was mid… the experience was not worth $55 a person at all. We wasted our time and money here, no thank you.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text: Terrible service. Waited over 30 minutes just to get drinks, and when I asked for water, the waiter gave blatant attitude and blamed being “swamped,” even though the restaurant was nearly empty. French fries and shrimp appetizer were really good but the atmosphere was unpleasant with a high-pitched annoying audible frequency. The waiter rolled his eyes multiple times, even after we thanked him for our food. When we asked for a to-go box, he literally put his head on the counter like he was irritated. I wouldn’t recommend!

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text:
Absolutely sublime food and service, could spend hours talking about the quality. Expect a bit of time to get a table and to get your food (due to the popularity of the place) but it is undeniably well worth the wait no doubt.

Service was smooth throughout and due to the wait time they even provided complimentary nachos and dips as a starter, which were brilliant. No doubt a brilliant resteratunt to come with your family or even friends.

title: Restaurante Fortuneño

title: Restaurante Fortuneño

title: Restaurante Fortuneño

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text:
Restauracja Fortuneno oferuje raczej poprawne, ale dość zachowawcze doświadczenie kulinarne. Zamówiony hamburger z frytkami był w porządku, choć sam burger nie wyróżniał się niczym szczególnym bez efektu „wow”, po prostu solidna klasyka. Podobnie kawa: espresso poprawne, lecz przeciętne, bez głębi smaku czy wyraźnego charakteru.
Na plus zasługują soki ananasowy był smaczny i orzeźwiający, natomiast marakuja niestety wyczuwalnie z syropu. Zdecydowanie największym atutem posiłku okazały się frytki serwowane z hamburgerem, fenomenalne: idealnie chrupiące, doskonale doprawione i naprawdę bardzo smaczne. To one zrobiły najlepsze wrażenie i pozostawiły po sobie najprzyjemniejsze wspomnienie z wizyty.

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text: The moment, we saw the most majestic decoration we have ever seen in a restaurant. The vibe was other worldly. When we were walking to our table, we could smell some unbelievably good smells coming from the kitchen. After we sat down, they brought to us an extensive menu, with an insane variety and selection of foods. The service was not particularly, but the food more than made up for it. The tomahawk steak was cooked to perfection, and they even put on a little show for us with fire. The staff was very kind, helpful, and funny. The dessert menu was also extensive, and there were so many different types of crepes. The crepes were some of the best crepes we have ever eaten, and they were amazing. So full of flavor! The bathrooms were also clean, and the music playing was definitely a nice touch. Overall unbelievable experience, the vibe was outstanding, and would definitely recommend.

title: Restaurante Fortuneño
text: The best acai bowl in La Fortuna, fresh, flavorful, and perfectly made. If you’re craving a refreshing and healthy treat, this place is definitely worth stopping by.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: Beautiful resort with a great view of the Arenal Volcano on a sunny day. Hike is about 35-40 min to the viewpoint. Food and service was amazing. The hot springs were the place to be.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: I did not stay at the hotel but got a day pass for the pools and it was awesome! The property is truly beautiful, with stunning flora around and beautiful pathways to walk. There are many different pools to choose from in a range of natural temperatures - I would encourage you to walk around and explore before you choose! Everything is beautiful and clean. The restaurant is also very good. The bartenders are friendly and hard working. They even made me a requested drink not on the menu (piña with albahaca)! There are 2 slides but to be honest they are quite dangerous. I did the smaller slide and it was extremely fast and I FLEW out of it with absolutely no control at any point. Perhaps adults are too heavy for them. The larger slide we didn't do because it is impossible to see the bottom from the top and there are no lifeguards so we were too worried about hitting someone if we didn't know they were there.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text:
I wanted to checkout the thermal spa. So I bought a day pass with lunch for $50. Totally loved different pools, not crowded, many pool to move. Even for a Sunday there were places in the pool area where you can enjoy the quiet time. Definitely worth checking out. Also there is a bar in one of pools for drinks and food.
The food was pretty decent. It was buffet on Sunday with my day pass. Overall enjoyed my time there.
If you walk to the end you pool path, you get a glimpse of the volcano.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text:
La experiencia del pasadia en este hotel fue excepcional. Cuesta a 55$ por persona con alimentación. Una variedad de actividades como senderismo. Caminamos 40 minutos por sus senderos hasta llegar al mirador del volcán Arenal. Muy mágico, místico esa vista del volcán con su laguito. Regresamos caminando y degustamos su menú a la carta. De entrada fue una ensalada del chef, como plato principal se pidió Chifrijol y de cierre un dulce de fresa. Muy pero muy deliciosa esa comida y la atención del que nos atendió fue muy top. Luego salimos el recorrido dónde tienen diferentes animalitos muy bien cuidados y disfrutamos de su lago acá en la entrada.
Recomendado este pasadia...

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: I was told by the staff that I had a day pass for the hotsprings and then when I arrived they would not honor the day pass even though i showed them the messages via WhatsApp with their staff

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text:
We stayed here for 3 nights last week. Overall the experience was positive.

Positives:
1. Grounds: Beautifully maintained grounds with excellent views.
2. Hot springs: There are 8 pools to choose from . Each pool is clearly identified with temperature and depth.
3. Wildlife: lots of wildlife to observe around the property.
4. Arenal views (if you are lucky and it is a clear sky!) from all over the property with an incredible view point only 2km up the resort.
5. Vastness of the property: The way the property is laid out, in small building over several acres of land surrounded by forest, makes it feel a lot less crowded and peaceful.
6. Breakfast: Amazing breakfast with options for everyone. Delicious!
7. Rooms were newly renovated and clean

Negative:
1. Could use better air circulation to remove humidity from the room without turning on the AC (our room would reach 18C every day, since AC unit was only way to control humidity).
2. Wish hot water temperature was warmer.

Summary:
We would stay here again if we were in La Fortuna. The prices are reasonable and property never felt crowded.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: We had a nice 3 day visit to this Resort when doing activities around LaFortuna.. We were pleasantly surprised at how nice it is!.. A bit "Disneyland feel", and that was just what we needed at the end of a 2 week Vacation 🤗

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: A great experience to stay at this very exotic place. One of the best placeto trave in Costa Rica.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text:
This hotel is well kept and nicely decorated.  Our room is beyond spacious due to there is a pull-out bed beneath our two queen beds.  I would like to have an additional loveseat or just one more chair in the room.  It is kind of odd you almost have to sit on your bed all the time.

There is hot spring in the hotel and it seems a major attraction.  We do enjoy.

There is only one onsite restaurant.  So we had all our lunch and dinner there.  The food is good and they do have a long list to choose from.

Overall the staff are professional and helpful.  We had a few “oh, that is what you mean” moments but if we could only speak Spanish well.

I would say the location to the nearby town is bit of far.  I may select a resort has more easier access to the town if there is a next time.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: We got a lot of "bang for our buck here". Our room was large and clean, we had amazing views of the volcano, there were enough Hot Springs to keep us happy for the 24 hours we were there, and breakfast was delicious. We were very satisfied for what was offered for the price we paid.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: This hotel is nice. I would bring children here. If I wanted a fun swim up bar/tropical trip with my friends I would probably come here. If you’re looking for a detox and Hot Springs, I would Not come here. The property is gorgeous.There’s lots of wildlife the rooms are really nice. The included breakfast is excellent. I would not eat at the restaurant for dinner—Just grab a burger or pizza at the pool bar.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: Wir hatten ein paar grandiose Tage im Hotel Los Lagos. Sehr nettes Personal, Zimmer größer als gebucht, eine phantastische Aussicht, Parkplatz für den Mietwagen direkt vor der Tür, das Frühstück sehr lecker und das Beste: die heißen Quellen! Unfassbar schön, inkl. Poolbar... Wir kommen gerne wieder

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: We stayed 3 nights. The room was spacious and beds were comfortable. Staff were all friendly and helpful. The amenities were great - pool, hot springs, gardens, walk to observation point. This hotel was recommended to us because of its amenities. It isn't in the main town but it was easy to Uber. Saw some wildlife on the property. Breakfast was delicious. I would stay here again.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: We only experience the thermal pools- beautiful. And great staff

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: BEWARE. This is such a dangerous place. I broke my ankle on a water slide and we heard of someone dying on the zip line. The staff did not care at all & tried to blame me for my injury? Then they didn’t even take me to a hospital, but an urgent care I had to pay out of pocket and barely did an xray. AWFUL AWFUL AWFUL DANGEROUS PLACE. Do yourself a favor and book ANYWHERE ELSE

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: Excellent hotel and hot springs. The restaurant and pool bar are great and prices are reasonable.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: this is a great place with the indoor hot pools, the cold pools with slides, as well as the numerous activities you can do from there. we tried the zip lining and it was amazing with the great staff. this place is highly recommended in the area!

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort

Challenging part of getting shuttle to go back/forth between room and front desk. You have to call from your room to get it and during new year party at resort we had to wait for 90 minutes to get it.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: Hermoso

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text:
We are a family of five with children between 6 and 18. Stayed here in room 203 for two nights. Very happy with the place. The view from our balcony overlooking the plains was spectacular. Very nice room and in very good shape. Extra plus to the restaurant staff who has a very high level of service. They really want to make your stay pleasant.
Another plus is for the food. Very good and tasty indeed. One of the best restaurants we visited in Costa Rica during our two week stay. Just sitting in the restaurant looking up towards the volcano and the jungle/hotel/waterfalls is a very nice experience in itself.

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: Great resort, beautiful and well maintained territory, awesome waterpark, very convenient location

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: We just got home from a three nights stay at Los Lagos.  We arrived in Costa Rica and stayed four nights on the beach and finished our trip up with ringing in the New Year in the jungle.  We had a family of seven ranging from 16 to 78 and every single one of us had the most amazing time. We enjoyed the hot springs, the staff at the swim up bar took great care of us and we were able to hike up to the observatory to view the volcano. We had such a good time!!

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text:
有一日溫泉的票，可以用WeChat 事先訂位，可以選擇晚餐或午餐，餐是buffet式的。
有免費的locker，溫泉區也有非常多的池，但是如果想要像亞洲那種溫泉這麼熱的池這邊應該沒有，這邊天氣也蠻熱的，可能也不需要這麼熱的池。

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: I can't say enough about this hotel. After a few rundown ones for the same price point in San José, this one blew us away. We were set way back so it was nice and quiet with a spectacular view of the volcano. We were lucky to have parking out front and a vehicle so we could wander around the property without waiting for the shuttle (which is loud I must say, due to the steepness!) The room and bathroom were in great shape and huge, and there were even trundles under each bed which was such a fun plan if you arrive with kids. (More hooks for towels would be great) The views are insane and you must go up to the view point (super long walk up, take the shuttle) and down to the lake. The pools are fantastic and well maintained. Make sure to walk up the path to the hot and cold side-by-side. Most people stick by the bar, so this area is much quieter. The breakfast is fantastic though a bit repetitive which we found all over CR, so normal. The staff are great. The souvenir shop has great prices - make sure to ask to pay in CRC, not USD. I got sick and we had to stay an extra night which even though a peak period, they gave us for a great deal. Highly recommend this spa hotel and area!!

title: Hotel Los Lagos Spa & Resort
text: Stayed here In august of 2025. This is a huge resort with incredible amenities. Numerous pools, swim up bar, water slide, etc. The drive there from the airport was crazy but was also an incredible way to see the country. This place has an breathtaking view of the volcano as well (when its not cloudy). This place is also right down the road from a natural hot spring which was incredible. Highly recommend!
